skill.name: analyze-project
skill.description: Read-only project analysis for deep learning repositories. It maps model structure, training and inference entrypoints, configs, and suspicious implementation patterns.
skill.summary: Map deep learning repositories and flag review targets without changing code.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the eval, shell backtick, weak crypto, path traversal, and obfuscation alerts are false positives in context. The real risk is medium because the included Python script reads a user-selected repository tree and writes analysis files to a user-selected output directory.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Repository File Reads
medium_findings.description: The helper resolves the supplied repository path, recursively scans files, and reads selected source and config files. This is expected for repository analysis, but users should run it only on repositories they intend to inspect.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly resolves user-provided paths and reads files from the target repository. No network exfiltration or command execution was found, so the risk is bounded to local filesystem exposure.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Directory Writes
medium_findings.description: The script writes Markdown and JSON analysis outputs to the requested output directory. This is legitimate behavior, but an unsafe output path could overwrite same-named report files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write paths are derived from the output directory argument and fixed report filenames. There is no evidence of hidden payload writes, but the filesystem side effect is real.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Dynamic Eval Alert Is a String Search
low_findings.description: Line 313 checks whether analyzed project text contains the literal .eval() and dropout terms. It does not call Python eval or execute user-controlled code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is inside a conditional substring check. There is no eval function call at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown formatting in skill text and generated reports. They are not Ruby or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious characters appear inside Python f-strings or Markdown documentation. No subprocess, shell, Ruby, or command interpolation API was found at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain natural language descriptions of Rigor Analyze or argparse help text. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are descriptive text, not crypto code. No weak algorithm usage is evidenced by these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Reference Is Not Path Traversal
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references a shared documentation file with a relative path. This is not code that reads arbitrary parent directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal pattern appears in Markdown documentation. The line does not perform filesystem access or sanitize bypass logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Obfuscation Heuristic on Normal Python File
low_findings.description: The script begins with a standard Python shebang and imports. No JSFuck-style obfuscation or encoded payload was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file header and imports are ordinary Python source. The heuristic appears to have misclassified bracket usage elsewhere in normal data structures.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Reads and Writes in Analysis Helper
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper reads from the requested repository and writes reports under the requested output directory. This pattern is appropriate for the skill but should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is explicit and central to the helper. It is not malicious, but it is a meaningful permission surface.

content.value_statement: Deep learning repositories can be hard to understand before changing code. This skill maps structure, entrypoints, configs, and review risks with a read-only workflow.
actual_capabilities: Maps training, inference, evaluation, model, and config entrypoint candidates.
Identifies task-relevant files for common deep learning project types.
Builds research maps, change maps, evaluation contracts, and status summaries.
Flags suspicious implementation patterns as heuristics for human review.
Reads optional JSON or YAML context to focus the analysis.
limitations: It does not patch repository code or apply fixes.
It does not run heavy training or evaluation jobs by default.
Its suspicious pattern output is heuristic and needs human confirmation.
It is designed for deep learning research repositories, not general office documents.

use_cases.title: Repository onboarding
use_cases.description: Quickly map a model repository before planning implementation work.

use_cases.title: Research reproduction review
use_cases.description: Identify training, evaluation, and config paths before reproducing a paper result.

use_cases.title: Pre-change risk review
use_cases.description: Find likely insertion points and fragile areas before modifying model code.

prompt_templates.title: Map a repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this deep learning repository. Summarize the model structure, main entrypoints, configs, and files I should read first.

prompt_templates.title: Find evaluation flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the evaluation path for this project. Identify likely metrics, benchmark scripts, expected outputs, and config dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a safe change
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze where a new model component could be inserted. Separate likely insertion points from protected evaluation code and risky assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit suspicious patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository for suspicious implementation patterns. Treat findings as heuristics and explain what evidence would confirm each one.

output_examples.input: Analyze a segmentation model repository before I edit it.
output: Identifies likely train, inference, evaluation, model, and config files.
Summarizes task-relevant data loader and metric files.
Lists conservative next reads before any code changes.

output_examples.input: Review a research repo for possible evaluation pitfalls.
output: Highlights evaluation commands, metrics, expected artifacts, and benchmark assumptions.
Separates confirmed facts from heuristic risk signals.
Produces a concise review path for human validation.

output_examples.input: Find safe insertion points for a new module.
output: Maps model, config, and training entrypoints that may accept the change.
Marks protected evaluation files and metric paths.

best_practices: Use it before editing unfamiliar model or training code.
Provide task, dataset, benchmark, and metric context when available.
Review generated risk notes manually before treating them as bugs.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for running tests or evaluations.
Do not ask it to patch code during the analysis step.
Do not treat heuristic warnings as confirmed defects without evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my repository?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for read-only analysis and report generation.

faq.question: What kinds of projects does it support best?
faq.answer: It works best on deep learning research repositories with model, training, evaluation, and config files.

faq.question: Can it run training jobs?
faq.answer: No. It avoids heavy execution and focuses on static project understanding.

faq.question: What files can it produce?
faq.answer: It can produce summary, risk, research map, change map, evaluation contract, and status reports.

faq.question: Are suspicious patterns confirmed bugs?
faq.answer: No. They are heuristics that require human review and supporting evidence.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
